GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.general_idea: Remove dependency on the deprecated WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter class.
GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.why_exists: WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter is deprecated in Spring Security 5.7+ and removed in Spring Security 6.0; extending it causes compilation errors or severe deprecation warnings in modern Spring Boot versions.

GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.general_idea: Replace the overridden configure(HttpSecurity) method with a SecurityFilterChain bean.
GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.why_exists: Without a SecurityFilterChain bean, Spring Security 6.0+ cannot configure HTTP security rules, leading to a runtime failure or missing security configuration.
GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.before:
@Override
protected void configure(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.after:
@Bean
public SecurityFilterChain filterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {

GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.general_idea: Add return statement to build the SecurityFilterChain.
GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.why_exists: The new bean method must return a SecurityFilterChain instance; omitting it causes a compilation error.

GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.general_idea: Remove the configure(WebSecurity) override for static resource ignoring.
GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.why_exists: The configure(WebSecurity) method is part of the deprecated WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter; in the new bean-based approach, static resources are typically handled via WebMvcConfigurer or default Spring Boot static resource handling, and keeping this override causes compilation errors against the removed adapter.

GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.general_idea: Replace super.userDetailsService() call with a direct instantiation of InMemoryUserDetailsManager.
GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.why_exists: The class no longer extends WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter, so super.userDetailsService() is undefined, causing a compilation error.
The class no longer extends WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter, so the import is unused and may cause compilation warnings or errors depending on strictness settings.
The configure(WebSecurity) method was removed, making this import unused.
Constructor injection is used instead of field injection, making the Autowired import unused.
The new filterChain method returns SecurityFilterChain, requiring the import to compile.
Spring Boot automatically injects dependencies into single constructors; the annotation is redundant and its removal is a cleanup.
The method is no longer overriding a method from WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter, so @Override is incorrect and causes a compilation error.

GapsWeb/src/main/java/com/jasonhhouse/ga.general_idea: Refactor chained method calls for readability.
